653c0d3fd2558b9e2148a59b.question: This musician collaborated with percussionist Will Clipman, guitarist William Eaton, and Tibetan flutist Nawang Khechog on the album In a Distant Place. This musician performed in a second movement duet with the soloist at the premier of Philip Glass’s Piano Concerto No. 2, “After (*) Lewis and Clark”. Over forty of this musician’s albums were produced by Canyon Records, including Earth Spirit and Canyon Trilogy, the only certified gold records by a Native American. For ten points, name this Navajo-Ute performer of the Native American flute.
653c0d3fd2558b9e2148a59b.question_sanitized: This musician collaborated with percussionist Will Clipman, guitarist William Eaton, and Tibetan flutist Nawang Khechog on the album In a Distant Place. This musician performed in a second movement duet with the soloist at the premier of Philip Glass's Piano Concerto No. 2, "After (*) Lewis and Clark". Over forty of this musician's albums were produced by Canyon Records, including Earth Spirit and Canyon Trilogy, the only certified gold records by a Native American. For ten points, name this Navajo-Ute performer of the Native American flute.

653c0d3fd2558b9e2148a5a3.question: The orchestra is split into five groups on different parts of the stage in this composer’s Archipelago S. This composer’s second collaboration with one director, after Dodes’ka-den, included the track “Hell’s Picture Scroll” in a battle scene; that movie is Akira Kurosawa’s (*) Ran. This composer secluded himself in a villa with Debussy scores in order to write “November Steps”. Stravinsky took this composer out to lunch after hearing his Requiem for Strings. For ten points, name this Messiaen-inspired classical Japanese composer.
653c0d3fd2558b9e2148a5a3.question_sanitized: The orchestra is split into five groups on different parts of the stage in this composer's Archipelago S. This composer's second collaboration with one director, after Dodes'ka-den, included the track "Hell's Picture Scroll" in a battle scene; that movie is Akira Kurosawa's (*) Ran. This composer secluded himself in a villa with Debussy scores in order to write "November Steps". Stravinsky took this composer out to lunch after hearing his Requiem for Strings. For ten points, name this Messiaen-inspired classical Japanese composer.
653c0d3fd2558b9e2148a5a3.answer_sanitized: Toru Takemitsu (accept names in either order)

653c0d3fd2558b9e2148a5a9.question: This composer called for members of the string orchestra to percussively strike their instruments in his Danzas de Panama. The first American opera performed by the New York City Opera was this composer’s work about the Haitian Revolution, (*) Troubled Island, which had a libretto written both by his future wife Verna Arvey and Langston Hughes. George Gershwin’s “I Got Rhythm” may have stolen from the scherzo of this composer’s first symphony, which features a banjo. For ten points, name this composer of the Afro-American Symphony.
653c0d3fd2558b9e2148a5a9.question_sanitized: This composer called for members of the string orchestra to percussively strike their instruments in his Danzas de Panama. The first American opera performed by the New York City Opera was this composer's work about the Haitian Revolution, (*) Troubled Island, which had a libretto written both by his future wife Verna Arvey and Langston Hughes. George Gershwin's "I Got Rhythm" may have stolen from the scherzo of this composer's first symphony, which features a banjo. For ten points, name this composer of the Afro-American Symphony.

653c0d40d2558b9e2148a5be.question: Later in life, this singer’s eye problems resulted in her wearing her signature sunglasses. Songs by this performer such as “Salou Qalby” were the result of collaboration with composer Riad Al-Sunbati and poet Ahmad Shawqi, and she sang translations of the Rubaiyat. This singer’s (*) “It’s Been a Long Time, O Weapon of Mine” was made the national anthem of her country, and she worked with the composer Abd al-Wahhab on songs such as “Enta Omry”. For ten points, what contralto is revered as the “Star of the East” in Egypt?
